intro: Schoenus sublateralis, also known as the sedge plant, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the family Cyperaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can aid in treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Schoenus sublateralis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Schoenus sublateralis is often used in decoctions, infusions, and tinctures to treat various ailments, and it is also used as a topical application to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
